title: Definitions
content: (1) 'body corporate' means a company as defined in Section 3 of the Companies Act, 1956, and includes LLP registered under this Act, LLP incorporated outside India, and a company incorporated outside India, but does not include corporation sole, co-operative society, and any other body corporate (not being company or LLP) which Central Government may notify; (d) 'contribution' means any payment to LLP in cash, promissory note, other agreement to contribute cash, tangible/intangible property, or other benefit to LLP including contracts for services performed or to be performed; (j) 'designated partner' means any partner designated as such pursuant to Section 7; (l) 'foreign LLP' means LLP formed, incorporated or registered outside India which establishes a place of business within India; (n) 'limited liability partnership' means partnership formed and registered under this Act; (p) 'limited liability partnership agreement' means any written agreement between partners of LLP or between LLP and its partners which determines mutual rights and duties of partners and their rights and duties in relation to LLP; (q) 'partner' means any person who becomes a partner in LLP in accordance with LLP agreement.

title: LLP to be Body Corporate
content: (1) Limited Liability Partnership is a body corporate formed and incorporated under this Act and is a legal entity separate from that of its partners. (2) LLP shall have perpetual succession. (3) Any change in partners of LLP shall not affect existence, rights or liabilities of LLP. LLP has separate legal personality distinct from its partners. Can sue and be sued in its own name. Property of LLP belongs to LLP and not to partners individually. LLP is not affected by death, insolvency, incapacity or retirement of a partner.

title: Non-Applicability of Partnership Act, 1932
content: The Indian Partnership Act, 1932 shall not apply to a limited liability partnership. This is significant because Partnership Act creates unlimited liability for partners whereas LLP provides limited liability. Partners of LLP are not partners within meaning of Partnership Act. However, general principles of partnership law may be applied where LLP Act is silent and principles are not inconsistent with LLP Act.

title: Partners
content: (1) Any individual or body corporate may be a partner in LLP. However, individual shall not be capable of becoming partner if: (a) person has been found to be of unsound mind by Court of competent jurisdiction and finding is in force; (b) person is undischarged insolvent; or (c) person has applied to be adjudicated as insolvent and application is pending. (2) Every LLP shall have at least two partners. (3) If number of partners reduced below two and LLP carries on business for more than six months with only one partner, that partner shall be liable personally for obligations of LLP incurred during that period. (4) Every LLP shall have at least two designated partners who are individuals and at least one of them shall be resident in India (stayed in India for not less than 120 days during financial year).

title: Eligibility to be Partners
content: On incorporation, persons who subscribed their names to incorporation document shall be partners of LLP and any other person may become partner of LLP by agreement with existing partners. No body corporate can be a designated partner - only individuals can be designated partners. Person shall give written consent to become partner. Partnership ceases upon death, or resignation in accordance with LLP agreement, or expulsion. Designated partner must have DPIN (Designated Partner Identification Number) obtained from Central Government.

title: Designated Partners
content: (1) Every LLP shall have at least two designated partners who are individuals and at least one of them shall be a resident in India. Explanation: For purposes of this section, 'resident in India' means person who has stayed in India for period of not less than 120 days during immediately preceding financial year. (2) In case of LLP in which all partners are bodies corporate or in which one or more partners are individuals and bodies corporate, at least two individuals who are partners of such LLP or nominees of such bodies corporate shall act as designated partners. (3) Subject to provisions of this section, if incorporation document does not specify designated partners or if at any time individual designated partners are less than required minimum, each partner shall be deemed to be designated partner. (4) Every designated partner shall: (a) be required to give consent in Form 9 to act as such; (b) be required to have DPIN.

title: Incorporation Document
content: For LLP to be incorporated: (a) two or more persons associated for carrying on a lawful business with a view to profit shall subscribe their names to an incorporation document; (b) incorporation document shall be filed with Registrar of the State in which registered office of LLP is to be situated; (c) incorporation document shall contain: (i) name of LLP; (ii) proposed business; (iii) address of registered office; (iv) name and address of each person who is to be partner of LLP; (v) name and address of each person who is to be designated partner of LLP; (vi) such other information as may be prescribed. Incorporation document shall be in electronic form prescribed by Central Government. Filing fee payable.

title: Incorporation by Registration
content: (1) When requirements of Section 11 are satisfied, Registrar shall retain incorporation document and register LLP by entering its name in register. (2) A certificate of incorporation signed by or on behalf of Registrar and authenticated shall be issued and shall state that: (a) LLP is incorporated by name mentioned therein; (b) LLP is LLP within this Act. (3) Certificate of incorporation is conclusive evidence that LLP is incorporated by name specified in certificate and that requirements of this Act have been complied with. On registration, LLP becomes body corporate with legal personality separate from that of its partners, with perpetual succession, capable of suing and being sued, of holding property in its own name.

title: Registered Office
content: (1) Every LLP shall have a registered office in India to which all communications and notices may be addressed. (2) LLP shall have registered office within 30 days of incorporation. (3) Every LLP shall ensure that its name and address of its registered office, registration number and statement that it is registered with limited liability are mentioned in all its business letters, letter heads, notices and all other official publications. (4) LLP shall furnish information of situation of its registered office to Registrar within 30 days of incorporation. Change of registered office within same State - intimate to Registrar within 30 days. Change to another State - requires Central Government approval.

title: LLP Agreement
content: (1) Save as otherwise provided by this Act, the mutual rights and duties of the partners of LLP inter se and mutual rights and duties of LLP and its partners shall be governed by LLP agreement between partners, or between LLP and its partners. (2) LLP agreement and any changes therein shall be filed with Registrar within 30 days of incorporation or date of such modification. (3) If LLP agreement is silent on any matter, the mutual rights and duties of partners and those of LLP and partners shall be governed by provisions specified in First Schedule. (4) First Schedule matters include: (a) all partners entitled to share equally in capital and profits; (b) partners to be indemnified for payments made and liabilities incurred in ordinary and proper conduct of business; (c) every partner may take part in management; (d) no partner shall be entitled to remuneration for acting in business; (e) no person may be introduced as partner without consent of all existing partners.

title: Partner as Agent
content: (1) Every partner of LLP is, for purpose of business of LLP, an agent of LLP, but not of other partners. (2) LLP is bound by anything done by partner in carrying on in usual way business of kind carried on by LLP - even if that partner had no authority to act for LLP in that matter, unless person dealing with LLP knows partner has no authority or does not know or believe him to be a partner. (3) LLP is not bound by anything done by partner if: (a) partner has in fact no authority to act for LLP in doing particular act; and (b) person with whom partner is dealing knows that partner has no authority or does not know or believe him to be a partner of LLP.

title: Extent of Liability of LLP
content: (1) LLP is not bound by anything done by partner in dealing with person if: (a) partner has in fact no authority to act for LLP in doing particular act; and (b) person with whom partner is dealing knows partner has no authority or does not know or believe him to be partner of LLP. (2) LLP is liable if partner of LLP is liable to any person as result of wrongful act or omission on his part in course of business of LLP, or with its authority. (3) An obligation of LLP whether arising in contract or otherwise shall be solely obligation of LLP. Partners of LLP shall not be personally liable directly or indirectly for obligations of LLP solely by reason of being partner of LLP.

title: Extent of Liability of Partner
content: (1) Partner is not personally liable, directly or indirectly for obligation of LLP solely by reason of being partner. (2) Nothing in this section shall affect personal liability of partner for his own wrongful act or omission, but partner shall not be personally liable for wrongful acts or omissions of any other partner of LLP. (3) Liability of LLP shall be met out of property of LLP. This is the fundamental protection provided by LLP structure - limited liability for partners. Partner's personal assets are protected from claims against LLP. However, personal liability continues for partner's own wrongful acts or omissions.

title: Maintenance of Books of Account
content: (1) LLP shall maintain proper books of account relating to its affairs for each year of its existence on cash or accrual basis of accounting which give true and fair view of state of affairs of LLP and explain transactions effected by LLP. (2) Books of account shall be kept at registered office or at such other place as partners may decide and shall be accessible to any partner for inspection during business hours. (3) LLP shall preserve books of account for at least eight years from date they are made. Section 35: Every LLP shall within 6 months of end of financial year file with Registrar: (a) Statement of Account and Solvency in Form 8; (b) Annual Return in Form 11.

title: Annual Return and Statement of Account
content: (1) Every LLP shall within 6 months of end of each financial year file with Registrar Statement of Account and Solvency in Form 8 and Annual Return in Form 11. (2) Statement of Account and Solvency shall be signed by designated partners. (3) Annual Return shall be signed by designated partner and contain: (a) address of registered office; (b) name, address, DPIN of each partner; (c) statement of total contribution received; (d) statement of total contribution received from each partner; (e) such other particulars as prescribed. Penalty for delay: Rs. 100 per day until default continues (max Rs. 1 lakh for LLP and Rs. 50,000 per partner for Statement of Account; Rs. 100 per day max Rs. 5 lakh for Annual Return).

title: Winding Up and Dissolution
content: LLP may be wound up by the Tribunal: (a) if LLP decides that LLP be wound up by Tribunal; (b) if for period of more than 6 months number of partners of LLP is reduced below two; (c) if LLP is unable to pay its debts; (d) if LLP has acted against interests of sovereignty and integrity of India, security of State, friendly relations with foreign States, or public order; (e) if LLP has made default in filing with Registrar Statement of Account and Solvency or Annual Return for any 5 consecutive financial years; (f) if Tribunal is of opinion that it is just and equitable that LLP be wound up. Creditors' voluntary winding up also available where partners by special resolution require LLP to be wound up voluntarily.

title: Circumstances for Inability to Pay Debts
content: LLP shall be deemed to be unable to pay its debts: (a) if creditor to whom LLP is indebted in sum exceeding Rs. 1 lakh has served on LLP by leaving at its registered office a demand requiring LLP to pay sum due, and LLP has for 21 days thereafter neglected to pay sum or to secure or compound for it to reasonable satisfaction of creditor; (b) if execution or other process issued on decree or order of any Court in favour of creditor against LLP is returned unsatisfied in whole or part; (c) if it is proved to satisfaction of Tribunal that LLP is unable to pay its debts, and in determining whether LLP is unable to pay its debts, Tribunal shall take into account contingent and prospective liabilities of LLP.
